claim: writeSummary is deterministic given the same row objects
evidence: header=scenario\tverdict\tkey_metric\tdetail\n; each field sanitized via replace(/\t/g,' ').replace(/\n/g,' '); tab-joined; fs.writeFileSync is synchronous and atomic on success

claim: SKIP diagnostic row embeds pid and reproducible explanation
evidence: key_metric=${name} owned by live daemon pid ${liveOwner.ownerPid}; detail=Connection skipped: a live single-writer owner (pid ${liveOwner.ownerPid}) holds the lease and bridging is disabled...

claim: EPERM-locked TSV fallback preserves prior-run pids creating reproducibility hazard
evidence: code==='EPERM' && fs.existsSync(SUMMARY_TSV) -> returns without writing -> prior-run TSV with stale pids remains

claim: owning pid is stable within a run if same daemon holds lease
evidence: liveOwnerForService reads on-disk lease at moment of connect failure; isPidAlive check; returns first alive pid from pidFields array

claim: TSV row structure is reproducible within session; pid VALUE is session-dependent and changes between sessions
evidence: mk-spec-memory SKIP with pid 57747 and mk-code-index SKIP with pid 69226; prior iteration observed different pids (48700 for code-index)
